item.title: Mesalamine in Treatment of Intestinal Inflammatory Diseases-Formulation-Driven Strategies for Targeted Colonic Therapy and Remission Maintenance.

item.title: Repeated intravenous weight-based ustekinumab maintenance as an optimization strategy for Crohn's disease patients with loss of response to standard therapy.

Currently, there is limited evidence on efficacy of repeated intravenous (IV) weight-based UST maintenan...

item.title: Renal AA amyloidosis associated with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: BMC nephrology - Renal AA amyloidosis is a rare but severe complication of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), associated with significant renal morbidity and poor prognosis. Data remain limited, particularly in unde...

item.title: Real-World Effectiveness of Upadacitinib for Perianal Crohn's Disease: A Multi-Center Retrospective Study.

item.title: From Bench to Bowel: A Review of Nanocarrier-based Drug Delivery Strategies in Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Recent advances in inflammation & allergy drug discovery - This review aims to discuss various nanocarrier systems for treatment in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), encompassing ulcerative colitis (UC) and Crohn's disease...

item.title: Microbial phosphoketolase promotes histone lactylation to improve anti-TNF therapy efficacy in inflammatory bowel disease.

item.title: Extended High-Dose Tofacitinib Induction Improves Clinical Response in Ulcerative Colitis: A Multicenter Cohort Study.
item.description: JGH open : an open access journal of gastroenterology and hepatology - Tofacitinib, an oral Janus Kinase (JAK) inhibitor, has demonstrated efficacy for ulcerative colitis (UC) in clinical trials, but the optimal dosing regimen remains unclear. This study aims to asses...

item.title: Computational design and in silico evaluation of mesalazine derivatives for enhanced treatment of inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: In silico pharmacology - Mesalazine (MES) is a first-line therapy for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD); however, its clinical use is limited by variable patient response, intolerance, and reduced efficacy in severe cases. ...

item.title: Comparative effectiveness of low versus high dose upadacitinib as maintenance treatment in patients with ulcerative colitis: a real-world cohort study from the United States.

item.title: Exclusive enteral nutrition and infliximab combination therapy are superior to monotherapy in inducing endoscopic response in active adult Crohn's disease.

item.title: Short-term efficacy and safety of infliximab biosimilar (HS626) in inducing remission of inflammatory bowel disease: A real-world single-center study.
